violations.text: ROP salmon were observed held at 43-46 degrees Fahrenheit in the cold hold sstation. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff voluntarily disposed of the ROP salmon during this inspection. -The ambient of the cold hold station observed at 40 degrees Fahrenheit and other TCS foods in unit were observed at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

ROP salmon observed still intact in reduced oxygen packaging in the cold hold station. ROP salmon observed at 43-46 degrees Fahrenheit. -Proper thawing of ROP frozen seafood products reviewed. -Staff voluntarily disposed of the ROP salmon during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
The condensate from the walk-in cooler is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

An employee was observed sprinkling parsley onto ready-to-eat stuffed cod with bare hands. -Proper glove use and no bare-hand contact with ready-to-eat foods reviewed. -PIC reheated stuffed cod to 201 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
The hand-washing sink in the bar area upstairs and downstairs were lacking paper towels. Staff provided paper towel during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Cup was observed in the hand-washing sink. Staff removed cup from hand washing sink at time of inspection. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the bar area upstairs and downstairs. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Staff provided soap at the time of this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. PIC moved can opener to 3-bay to wash, rinse and sanitize. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment shall sanitize in 3-bay sink until dishwasher is serviced and in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. PIC moved scoop to dish washer to be washed, rinsed and sanitized. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Gaskets at the cold hold unit were observed in poor repair. Gaskets shall be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Mop sink located in the (storage) area observed not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026
The condensate from the (walk-in cooler) was observed draining into a hand-washing sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The faucet to the 3 bay sink was observed to be missing a handle to control the water at the spout valve - The 3 bay sink was observed to be lacking drain stoppers Equipment is to be maintained in good repair/properly adjusted Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the sandwich prep unit gaskets were observed to have an accumulation of food residue and debris. Non-food-contact. surfaces are to be cleaned as often as necessary to preclude the buildup of debris Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2025

The floor in the ice machine area is constructed of unsealed cement, which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

At the time of inspection, the soda gun holder drain tube was observed to be missing at the bar area and dripping into the ice well below. The soda gun holder was relocated and ice was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. The dish machine was adjusted at the time of inspection and observed to be properly measuring at 50 to 100 ppm by end of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

At the time of inspection, foods in the sandwich prep unit were observed held at 43 to 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items observed at 45 degrees and higher were voluntarily disposed of, and food items observed at 43 to 44 degrees Fahrenheit were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The sandwich prep unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 43 degrees Fahrenheit with food temps observed at 43 to 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Call was made for service at the time of inspection. PIC is to work from small portions until repaired. - The faucet to the 3 bay sink was observed to be missing a handle to control the water at the spout valve - The 3 bay sink was observed to be lacking drain stoppers Equipment is to be maintained in good repair/properly adjusted New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the sandwich prep unit gaskets were observed to have an accumulation of food residue and debris. Non-food-contact surfaces are to be cleaned as often as necessary to preclude the buildup of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

The floor in the ice machine area is constructed of unsealed cement, which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
